item.title: Sturtevant's Ski-Ya-Later Sale: Up to 50% Off Ski and SnowboardGear
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Sturtevant's Ski Mart, 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA

PNW skiers and snowboarders—spring is the smartest time to upgrade! Sturtevant's \(part of Christy Sports\) is hosting the annual Ski-Ya-Later Sale from March 6-29, offering up to 50% off current-season skis, snowboards, boots, bindings, apparel, and more—perfect as spring storms bring late-season conditions and families plan ahead during spring break. Shop in-person at our Bellevue locations for expert tuning & repair, custom boot fitting, water sports rentals, and more: Sturtevant's – 13131 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.869.7669\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am- 6 pm Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.637.8958\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm Or head to Tacoma: Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 2220 So. 37th St., Tacoma, WA 98409 \(253.473.1134\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm. Great for families: Stop by for kids' fit checks, season rental info, and early purchases before fall pricing. All discounted items are final sale; selection varies by store. Also shop online at christysports.com or sturtevants.com. Upgrade your gear locally this spring—visit Sturtevant's Bellevue or Tacoma today. Dates: March 6 – March 29, 2026 \(Progressive discounts: Up to 30% off March 6-12 with the best selection; up to 40% off March 13-19; up to 50% off March 20-29 for the deepest savings\)

Event details

item.title: Daybreak Star Community 5K Run and Walk
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

Join the community for a series of monthly 5K runs and walks held at the historic Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center. This free event welcomes participants of all levels, from competitive runners to those who enjoy a leisurely walk through beautiful scenery. Event Schedule Events take place on the 3rd Sunday of each month. Please note the following upcoming dates: February 15th March 15th April 19th May 17th June 21st July 19th \(37th Annual Indian Days Powwow Weekend, starting at 9:00 AM\) Timing and Location Details Start Time: 10:00 AM Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center \(5011 Bernie Whitebear Way Seattle, WA 98199\) Meeting Point: Meet at the Powwow Grounds This event is FREE for all! Lace up your shoes and enjoy the scenic trails while connecting with the community! For more information about the venue and organizing group, please email communications@unitedindians.org

Event details

item.title: ToDos Bike Garage -- Bike Repair Co-op
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 11 AM

Come work on bikes on Sundays! Please call/text ahead \(323.926.8054\) to confirm ToDos Bike Garage will be open on a given Sunday. Volunteer run. ToDos Bike Garage is open for bike repair, tune-up, and flat fixes. We have all the basic bike tools and are ready to work on bikes together! We're happy to teach you how to maintain your bike, and help you get it working right. It's free to come work on your bike, and we do have some parts available, but you may need to buy your own parts if something comes up. Follow @todos\_garage on Instagram!

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Issaquah Alps Trail Running Club #227
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Squak Mtn- Bullitt Fireplace Trailhead, Mountainside Dr SW, Issaquah, WA

Meet at the Bullitt Fireplace Trailhead on Mountainside drive. There's plenty of parking along Mountainside drive. Bring a headlamp for the run. Join us after the run for our weekly potluck. Bring a potluck dish with a serving utensils, and your own dish and utensil. Route: Main Route: This route is 6mi with 1600ft of gain. The run starts with most the vert up to the microwave, and heads down Chybinski trail. Route: https://www.strava.com/routes/3290114707713132698 Strava Event: https://www.strava.com/clubs/993715/group\_events/3462646694539309928

item.title: Trail Explorers - Kelly's Brook Trail
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Farmer's Market: 245 Freshwater Road. Upper parking lot, 4749 Ballard Ave NW, Seattle, WA

Join the Trail Explorers on Wednesday, March 18, at 10:30 a.m. They will be exploring Kelly's Brook Trail, and the meeting location is the Farmer’s Market: 245 Freshwater Road. Upper parking lot. This trail is 4 km and will take approximately 60-75 minutes to complete. It is rated a 3 on the difficulty scale \(on a 1-5 scale, with 1 being the easiest and 5 being the hardest. For more information please visit: https://www.stjohns.ca/en/recreation-culture/trail-explorers.aspx This initiative provides a wonderful opportunity to explore the beautiful trails throughout the city, promoting health, wellness, and community connection. Please contact healthyprograms@stjohns.ca for further information.

Event details

item.title: Audubon Bird Walk
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Learn more about the birds at the Bellevue Botanical Garden. Join Eastside Audubon for a monthly walk that explores the birds and their habitat in the Garden. This one-mile walk will be on trails with various surfaces and may include gravel, mulch, or pavement. Please dress for the weather and wear comfortable walking shoes. Parking in the lot is free. From the parking lot enter the garden through the breezeway and turn left. Meet your guide in the covered Gathering Place. This walk is for any level of birder and no experience is necessary. Bring binoculars if you have them. There will be some available to loan for the walk. Each walk is $5 per person. Registration is required.

Event details

item.title: Saint Edward State Park Restoration Work Party
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Do you want to give back to Saint Edward State Park? Are you looking for meaningful ways to support local natural spaces? Come join us! Join us for a day of stewardship and discovery at Saint Edward State Park! The park is home to rich flora, fauna, and history and with increased visitation, it needs our collective care more than ever. All ages 8 \+ welcome; no experience needed. Work happens rain or shine, please dress for the weather. What to bring \(required\): Rain Jacket Closed toe shoes Water Hand pruners or loppers \(we have some but could always use more\) Gardening Gloves \(we will provide extra if you do not have your own\)

Event details

item.title: Mindfulness Walk | Pioneer Park
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 11 AM

Mindfulness Walk Taking inspiration from the Japanese idea of shinrin-yoku \(forest bathing\), we will be hosting a series of mindfulness walks in Pioneer Park. Mindfulness walking in the forest has shown to have a myriad of benefits from improving heart health to reducing stress \(https://www.npr.org/2023/08/22/1195337204/a-guide-to-forest-bathing\). Forest Steward Marilyn Cornwell \(https://www.soulscapes.us/\) will be lea ding an hour-long guided walk through the park. Please join us if your cur ious about mindfulness, want to learn more about and experience healing powers of being in nature, and/or if you want to deepen your connection with Pioneer Park and others. When: Thursday, March 19th, 11am-12pm Where: Pioneer Park NW, Meet at the corner of Island Crest Way and SE 68th St. Follow VOLUNTEER EVENT Signs What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes and shoes you will comfortable walking and standing in, as well as water. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/9XTJA

Event details

item.title: Forterra Fridays: March 2026
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Crestview Park, 16200 42nd Avenue South, Tukwila, WA

Forterra Fridays: March 2026 Friday, Mar. 20, 2026, 1:00 pm to 3:30 pm We’re excited to invite you to volunteer with us at Crestview Park, a beloved community gathering space in Tukwila featuring dog play areas, trails, and natural landscapes. Join us on March 20, from 1:00-3:30 PM, to restore essential habitat by removing ivy and Himalayan blackberries that threaten our local native trees, shrubs, and groundcover. Established native plants benefit local wildlife, improve water quality, and enhance air quality.  You will also gain valuable insights into ecological restoration and our innovative approach to land conservation. We’ll start with a walk of the site and have restoration professionals on hand to answer questions about plants, restoration, and habitat management.  What we are doing: Crestview Park provides critical habitat restoration opportunities alongside its popular amenities. By removing invasive ivy and blackberries, we ensure that native plants can thrive, supporting the park’s ecological health while maintaining the community spaces that make this park so valued by Tukwila residents.  What to bring: Please dress for the weather \(in layers to stay warm/dry\). Snacks, drinks, tools, and gloves will all be provided. Where to park: Park in the Crestview Park parking lot \(16200 42nd Avenue South\). Signs direct you from the parking lot to where we will meet. Date and Time: Friday, March 20, 2026, 1:00 –3:30 PM  Register and learn more here.

Event details

item.title: Green Issaquah: Restoration Event at Park Pointe
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: SE Evans St, Issaquah, WA

Join the Issaquah Alps Trails Club and Green Issaquah on the third Saturday of each month for invasive species removal events at Park Pointe! We will be removing invasive species such as Himalayan blackberry bushes to prepare for our fall planting event. These invasive plants inhibit the growth of native plants, and removing them will ensure the longevity and health of our newest additions. This event is part of our ongoing efforts to help meet the Issaquah Climate Action Plan goal of 55% tree canopy cover by 2035. When and Where Date & Time: Saturday, March 21st, 2025, 9 AM - Noon Where: Park Pointe Where to Park Parking for Park Pointe is available along SE Evans Street, which is overflow for Issaquah High School and the parking for the Sportsman Club \(see inset picture map below\). Click here for parking directions. Where to Meet We will be meeting at the end of the dirt road at the trailhead. What to Bring Bring your own gloves if you have them. There will be gloves available for those who don't have their own. Please dress in layers and in clothing you don't mind getting dirty. Closed-toed shoes are required. Long sleeves and pants, especially denim, are strongly recommended. Bring a full water bottle and snacks. Don't forget to bring your sunscreen! Point of Contact Anne Newcomb \(anne@annenewcomb.net\) and Matt LaBelle \(mattlabelle@hotmail.com\), your work party leaders. Registration required. You can check out our Event FAQs on our website.

Event details

item.title: DeBays Slough Planting Party
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Description Join us for planting at the Debays Slough Reserve on Saturday, March 21st. Join us 9am-noon to continue restoring the riparian zone of DeBay's Slough off Francis Road in Mount Vernon. Please dress for the weather; we will provide work gloves and tools, and closed-toe shoes or mud boots. Click the link to register today!

Event details

item.title: SAM Body + Mind: Activity Days
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Olympic Sculpture Park, 2901 Western Ave, Seattle, WA

Experience a full spectrum of grounding, creative, and wellness-focused practices with SAM Body \+ Mind: Activity Days. Designed to rejuvenate both inner and outer worlds, each session offers a mix of mindful movement, quiet reflection, hands-on creativity, and holistic care. Participate in gentle Qigong to awaken your energy, enjoy replenishing moments with acupuncture, or settle into the calm of silent reading in a peaceful shared space. Explore your artistic side with natural dye and art-making workshops that nourish connections to yourself and the natural world. Come for one activity or stay for the full experience-each offering invites you to slow down, reconnect, and care for your body and mind. 8:30 am | Doors open 9-10 am | Tai Chi/Qigong With Sensei Jody Garcia 10 am-1 pm | Workshops Art workshop with NW Expressive and Creative Arts Institute Natural dyes workshop with Earth Corps Acupuncture with Dr. Yanzi Jiang, Dr. Milo Jackson, and Dr. Nikeeta Shah noon-2 pm | Musical performance Ambient Vinyl DJ set performed by DJ Raica 1-2 pm | Silent reading With West Seattle Book Club Food and drinks Food and beverages will be available for purchase from Shooby Doo Catering in the PACCAR Pavilion. Presenting Sponsor

Event details

item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. item.title: South Lynnwood Park Stewardship Event
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Scriber Lake Park, 5562 196th St SW, Lynnwood, WA

City of Lynnwood Parks welcomes volunteers of all abilities who want to contribute to the restoration and conservation of our parks in South Lynnwood Neighborhood. The goal of each event is to provide a safe, enjoyable, and effective hands-on experience for you to help improve the health of your urban parks. These monthly events, which will be held on the third weekend of each month, will involve removing invasive plants, help with restoration plantings, and picking up garbage. Events are 100% outdoors and will generally be held ‘rain or shine’ but may be canceled or relocated if weather or site conditions are unsafe such as high winds, flooding, snow, lightning, or unsafe air quality \(smoke\).

Event details

item.title: NPA March Mania Plant Sale
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

March is a great time to buy plants from a select group of local small nurseries and specialty growers who sell an amazing collection of hard-to-find and old-time favorite perennials, shrubs, ferns, grasses and more. Your support helps these growers thrive and prosper, and in return they can grow even more special plants for our gardens! This is a ‘Farmer’s Market Style’ plant sale where you purchase plants directly from each seller – bring your own small wagon/cart to carry your purchases back to your car. The plant sale features plants from local growers. Visit the NPA Border after shopping! Email for more info.

item.title: Mindfulness Walk | Pioneer Park
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 11 AM

Mindfulness Walk Taking inspiration from the Japanese idea of shinrin-yoku \(forest bathing\), we will be hosting a series of mindfulness walks in Pioneer Park. Mindfulness walking in the forest has shown to have a myriad of benefits from improving heart health to reducing stress \(https://www.npr.org/2023/08/22/1195337204/a-guide-to-forest-bathing\). Forest Steward Marilyn Cornwell \(https://www.soulscapes.us/\) will be lea ding an hour-long guided walk through the park. Please join us if your cur ious about mindfulness, want to learn more about and experience healing powers of being in nature, and/or if you want to deepen your connection with Pioneer Park and others. When: Tuesday, March 24th, 11am-12pm Where: Pioneer Park SE, Meet at the corner of Island Crest Way and SE 68th St. Follow VOLUNTEER EVENT Signs What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes and shoes you will comfortable walking and standing in, as well as water. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/9XTJA Opens in new window PDF Download Word Download Excel Download PowerPoint Download Document Download

item.title: Squak Mountain Celebration Hike
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Squak Mtn- Bullitt Fireplace Trailhead, Mountainside Dr SW, Issaquah, WA

Join IATC Vice President Selena Eon for an afternoon hike on Squak Mountain to celebrate all of the hard work volunteer crews have put in to restore these trails in the wake of the 2024 bomb cyclone. Throughout 2025, over 130 IATC volunteers worked to remove debris and restore tread to trails on Squak over the course of 25 work parties. We’ll meet at Mountainside Drive Trailhead \(also known as Bullitt Fireplace Trailhead\) and work our way up to Old Griz, where a substantial portion of the 2025 trail work took place. We’ll also view the landslide sites on Phil’s Creek before heading down the Eastside Trail back to the trailhead. Trails: Mountainside Drive Trail, Old Griz, Phil’s Creek, Eastside Trail Meeting Location: Mountainside Drive Trailhead Date & Time: Wednesday, March 25th, 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM Duration: 5 hours Distance: 5 miles Elevation Gain: 1300 feet NOTE: This hike includes 1300 feet of elevation gain over 5 miles, making it a moderate to difficult hike.

item.title: Restoration Event | Pioneer Park
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 4 PM

Pioneer Park Join us for an afternoon work party in Pionee r Park. This beautiful habitat is being threatened by non-native weeds, such as ivy and blackberry. Ivy plays a big role in local habitat destructi on by pulling down trees, shading out native plants, and reducing biodiversity. Join in our efforts and help to create a healthier forest. A ll ages welcome, and no experience necessary! Gloves, tools, water, an d small snacks provided. When: Thursday, March 26th, 4pm-6pm Where: Pioneer Park SE, Meet at the corner of Island Crest Way and SE 68th St. Follow VOLUNTEER EVENT Signs What to bring: Dress for the weather in cloth es you don't mind getting dirty \(think: long pants, boots or sneakers\). Bring water. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/CWRCR

Event details

item.title: PACIFIC PLACE ‘SWEET SIXTEEN SHOWDOWN' WATCH PARTY FOR MARCH MADNESS FANS
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: PACIFIC PLACE, 600 Pine Street, Seattle, WA

Pacific Place, a premier five-story shopping center in downtown Seattle \(www.pacificplaceseattle.com\), invites basketball fans to experience the excitement of the Sweet Sixteen round of the NCAA March Madness tournament during its “Sweet Sixteen Showdown” watch party from 5 p.m. to 7 p.m. on Thursday, March 26th. Guests can watch the games live on the center’s brand-new 40-foot by 70-foot LED screen – capable of showing up to three games simultaneously – before testing their skills at a basketball Pop-a-Shot challenge. The event will also feature a game-day pretzel bar, a cash bar serving signature cocktails, and a basketball-themed photo opportunity for fans to capture the moment. This event is free and open to the public. While reservations are not required, space is limited and guests are encouraged to reserve a spot in advance at https://bit.ly/4b4q5Yk. To learn more, click here or visit https://pacificplaceseattle.com/events.

Event details

Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

item.title: Pilates for Gardeners
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by Jo Anne Iaciofano. Grab your mat, kick off your shoes, and join this Pilates-inspired class to improve body movement, core strength, and muscle control. This beginner’s friendly class, using mind-body connection and breath, will take you through a series of exercises with an emphasis on technique and fluidity. We will focus on those areas most stressed during gardening by using your core to power the muscles increasing your energy and endurance. All levels are welcome. Wear comfortable clothing and be prepared to workout barefoot. Please bring a yoga or Pilates mat, towel, and water. This class will take place in the Aaron Education Center.  $17.50 BBGS Member/$25 Non-Member.

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. item.title: Restoration Event | Pioneer Park
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

Pioneer Park Clean Up! Join us for a Forest Steward led wo rk party in Pioneer Park SE. For this work party volunteers will be removi ng ivy that is blanketing the forest floor. Removing ivy helps to increase the biodiversity of park by making room for native plants to sprout up. I f left unchecked, ivy will grow over our native plants and eventually pull them to the ground. Your help in removing ivy will play a pivotal role i n creating a healthier future for the park! No experience necessary, all ages welcome. Please note: we require participants aged 14 and under to volunteer with a parent or guardian at a ratio of 1 adult to 3 kids. When: Saturday, March 28th, 10am-12pm Where: Pioneer Park SE, a map will be provided for directions to the specific work site. What to bring : Dress for the weather in clothes you don't mind getting dirty \(think: lo ng pants, boots or sneakers\). Bring water. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/CWRCR

Event details
